mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, with a decayed Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained decline in the index of industrial production signals capacity constraints and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in late-cycle regimes or shows no predictive power when isolated to expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Inflation increases consistently coincide with positive equity returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product exceeding 0.5 percent confirms economic contraction, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during confirmed GDP contractions, with forward returns exceeding zero in more than 70 percent of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no incremental predictive value over individual series, with interaction term coefficients statistically insignificant at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Following a macroeconomic shock, a rapid rebound in industrial production indicates restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds fail to predict short-term equity gains in recovery phases, with the signal's Sharpe ratio remaining below 0.5.
